comment_text.sn21.7:1.3: This event was also recorded at AN 4.48, and his verses at Thag 2.45.

root_text.sn21.7:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn21.7:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā visākho pañcālaputto upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti, poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāya.
root_text.sn21.7:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena upaṭṭhānasālā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn21.7:2.3: “ko nu kho, bhikkhave, upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāyā”ti?
root_text.sn21.7:2.4: “Āyasmā, bhante, visākho pañcālaputto upaṭṭhānasālāyaṁ bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti, poriyā vācāya vissaṭṭhāya anelagalāya atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāyā”ti.
root_text.sn21.7:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ visākhaṁ pañcālaputtaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn21.7:3.3: sādhu kho tvaṁ, visākha, bhikkhū dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi …pe… atthassa viññāpaniyā pariyāpannāya anissitāyā”ti.
root_text.sn21.7:4.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn21.7:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn21.7:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Visākha, Pañcāli’s son, was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants in the assembly hall with a Dhamma talk. His words were polished, clear, articulate, expressing the meaning, comprehensive, and independent.
translation_text.sn21.7:2.1: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to the assembly hall. He sat down on the seat spread out,
translation_text.sn21.7:2.3: “Mendicants, who was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants in the assembly hall with a Dhamma talk?”
translation_text.sn21.7:2.4: “Sir, it was Venerable Visākha, Pañcāli’s son.”
translation_text.sn21.7:3.1: Then the Buddha said to Visākha:
translation_text.sn21.7:3.3: It’s good that you educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire the mendicants in the assembly hall with a Dhamma talk, with words that are polished, clear, articulate, expressing the meaning, comprehensive, and independent.”
translation_text.sn21.7:4.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn21.7:4.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn21.7:5.1: “Though an astute person is mixed up with fools,
translation_text.sn21.7:5.2: they don’t know unless he speaks.
translation_text.sn21.7:5.3: But when he speaks they know,
translation_text.sn21.7:5.4: he’s teaching the state free of death.
translation_text.sn21.7:6.1: He should speak and illustrate the teaching,
translation_text.sn21.7:6.2: holding up the banner of the seers.
translation_text.sn21.7:6.3: Words well spoken are the seers’ banner,
translation_text.sn21.7:6.4: for the teaching is the banner of the seers.”
